item_text: This file ranks the app's current features by pilot essentiality first while also factoring in value implementation effort privacy/security burden and likely time savings.
how important the feature is to making the pilot actually useful
how useful the feature is during real operations
how much implementation/support/maintenance burden it adds
how much identity, personnel, or sensitive operational data it touches
rough estimate of the labor/time the feature could save if adopted
Those are still valuable but they are not as clean or easy as the first-wave pilot features.

potential_time_savings: ~1,000+ hrs/yr across line shifts (~$14k+/yr at $14/hr), using a conservative 5 min saved per worker per shift
reason: Most essential pilot feature because it is the core shift tool, already broadly useful, and combines high value with low effort and low rollout risk.

current_app_area: Dashboard > Workflow > Line > Supervisor > Jobs/Secondaries/Deep Clean
potential_time_savings: ~150-300 supervisor hrs/yr through faster assignment, checkoff, and fewer in-person follow-ups
reason: Core pilot feature with strong shift value. It speeds supervision immediately and makes the pilot more useful to leadership on day one.

potential_time_savings: ~100-200 hrs/yr from fewer questions, faster lookups, and less supervisor interruption
reason: High pilot value, very low effort, and broadly useful across many shifts. This is one of the safest high-return pilot features.

potential_time_savings: ~100-180 hrs/yr from faster item lookup for workers plus reduced interruption of supervisors and experienced staff
reason: Very pilot-friendly and highly practical. Even small lookup savings compound quickly across many workers and shifts.

potential_time_savings: ~60-120 hrs/yr in prep and repeated explanation time, plus better training consistency
reason: Not as operationally critical as workflows, but very pilot-friendly, low effort, and useful every day.

potential_time_savings: Indirect savings only, but it removes login/setup friction across every pilot test session
reason: Not a big steady-state labor saver, but highly essential to the pilot itself because it reduces friction and makes rollout/testing practical.

potential_time_savings: ~100-220 hrs/yr in trainer time through better structure, faster checkoff, and less repeated verbal instruction
reason: Strong pilot feature once stable because it directly helps lead trainers move faster and be more thorough.

potential_time_savings: ~60-140 hrs/yr plus reduced food waste from fewer mistakes rework and burned product
reason: Good pilot feature because it helps new workers and can save both labor and product waste when cooks need quick answers.

potential_time_savings: ~150-250 supervisor hrs/yr from faster end-of-shift reporting and more consistent structure
reason: Very valuable, but slightly less pilot-essential than the core shift flows because it is more process-heavy and touches stored records.

potential_time_savings: ~40-100 hrs/yr from fewer repeated verbal reminders and cleaner communication
reason: Useful in a pilot, but not as essential as workflows and references.

potential_time_savings: ~80-180 hrs/yr from eliminating handwritten point slips binder handling and delayed manual follow-up
reason: This is genuinely high value, but it ranks lower because it is higher effort, higher sensitivity, and less essential than the core pilot surfaces.

potential_time_savings: ~60-140 leadership hrs/yr from centralized report review instead of paper texts or verbal handoff
reason: Valuable once reports exist, but secondary to drafting and the core pilot operations.

potential_time_savings: ~60-120 hrs/yr by removing manual approval chasing and scattered review steps
reason: High value, but heavier and more sensitive than the simpler pilot features above it.

potential_time_savings: ~40-100 hrs/yr from eliminating binder lookup and rebuilding old point history by hand
reason: Important long-term value, but not as pilot-essential as the live operational tools.

potential_time_savings: ~40-100 hrs/yr from reducing signature chasing and long-delayed acknowledgment follow-up
reason: Useful, but it depends on the broader points workflow and carries the same rollout complexity.

potential_time_savings: ~120-250 hrs/yr once stable through less retraining and cleaner task execution
reason: Potentially very valuable, but still less essential than the line-centered pilot surfaces.

potential_time_savings: ~100-220 hrs/yr once stable through reduced retraining and missed cleaning steps
reason: High operational value, but less central to the current pilot than line workflows.

potential_time_savings: ~60-120 hrs/yr once stable through faster training and better checkoff
reason: Useful, but it depends on dishroom maturity and is less essential to the immediate pilot.

potential_time_savings: ~20-60 hrs/yr from faster publishing versus manual relaying
reason: Helpful, but not especially pilot-essential and not a top value/effort win.

potential_time_savings: ~20-60 hrs/yr from quicker leftover and setup lookups
reason: Useful niche reference, but narrower than the top pilot features.

potential_time_savings: ~20-50 hrs/yr through faster close/checkoff lookups
reason: Helpful, though more limited in scope than the broader reference and workflow tools.

potential_time_savings: ~10-40 hrs/yr from occasional rotation lookup savings
reason: Good to have, but narrow and not pilot-essential.

potential_time_savings: ~10-30 hrs/yr, mostly for newer workers and occasional wayfinding questions
reason: Helpful onboarding aid, but one of the lower-return pilot features.

potential_time_savings: Little to no direct labor savings

item_text: The first-wave pilot should still center on the features that make the app obviously useful with low rollout friction.
Points and reporting are still high-value features with real labor-saving potential even though they rank lower than the simplest pilot features.
